docs.title: Welcome to B-Gyro
docs.text: "Meet B-Gyro: our attempt at an operating system inspired by the gyroscope's stability... except ours wobbles more than it balances. Think of it as a gyroscope that moonlights as a boomerang—it keeps coming back with more bugs to fix!" -- by 0rayn and faithByte

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• 32-bit operating system
  • Developed by 0rayn and faithByte
  • Debugging system using serial communication.
  • partial-support for Ansi escape codes, for colored text.
  • Minimalistic shell, with basic commands to interact with the system.

docs.title: About Us
docs.text: We are a team of passionate developers working on creating a unique operating system. Our goal is to learn, innovate, and have fun along the way.

docs.title: Get Started
docs.text: To get started with B-Gyro, check out our documentation.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text: For any inquiries or feedback, feel free to reach out to us at b.gyro.os@gmail.com

docs.title: Welcome to B-Gyro
docs.text: this document is a guide to help you get started with B-Gyro. If you have any questions or need help, feel free to reach out to us at b.gyro.os@gmail.com

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Prerequisites
  • Play-Test
  • kernel API

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Before you begin, ensure you have met the following requirements: - docker and docker-compose installed on your machine. - qemu-system-x86_64 installed on your machine.

docs.title: play-test:
docs.text:
To play-test B-Gyro, follow these steps:

  1. just clone the repository and run the following command: 
    make
  2. To run the operating system, run the following command: 
    make run

docs.text:
• Ports IO
  • Serial Communication

docs.title: Functions:
docs.text:
• portByteIn
  • portByteOut
  • portWordIn
  • portWordOut
  • ioWait

docs.title: portByteIn
docs.text:
> Syntax: 

uint8_t portByteIn(uint16_t port);

> Description: Reads a byte from the specified port.

docs.title: portByteOut
docs.text:
> Syntax: 

void portByteOut(uint16_t port, uint8_t data);

> Description: Writes a byte to the specified port.

docs.title: portWordIn
docs.text:
> Syntax: 

uint16_t portWordIn(uint16_t port);

> Description: Reads a word (16 bit) from the specified port.

docs.title: portWordOut
docs.text:
> Syntax: 

void portWordOut(uint16_t port, uint16_t data);
> Description: Writes a word (16 bit) to the specified port.

docs.title: ioWait
docs.text:
> Syntax: 

void ioWait(void);

> Description: Wait a very small amount of time (1 to 4 microseconds, generally). Useful for implementing a small delay for PIC remapping on old hardware.

docs.title: Functions
docs.text:
• initSerial
  • isTransmitEmpty
  • serialPutChar

docs.title: initSerial
docs.text:
> Syntax: 

int initSerial(void);

> Description:

Initializes the serial communication using the UART protocol.

This function configures the serial port with the following settings: - Disables all serial interrupts. - Enables DLAB to set the baud rate divisor. - Sets the baud rate to 38400. - Configures the port for 8 bits, no parity, and one stop bit. - Enables and clears the FIFO with a 14-byte threshold. - Enables IRQs and sets RTS/DSR. - Tests the serial chip in loopback mode. - Verifies the serial chip functionality. - Sets the serial port to normal operation mode if the chip is functional.

Returns 0 on success, 1 if the serial chip is faulty.

docs.title: isTransmitEmpty
docs.text:
> Syntax: 

int isTransmitEmpty(void);

> Description:

Checks if the transmit buffer is empty.

This function returns 1 if the transmit buffer is empty, otherwise it returns 0.

docs.title: serialPutChar
docs.text:
> Syntax: 

uint8_t serialPutChar(char c);

> Description:

Sends a character over the serial port.

This function places the character c into the transmit buffer and returns 1. If the buffer is full, it waits until the buffer is empty and then places the character in the buffer.

docs.title: Note
docs.text: All these functions use the COM1 port for serial communication.
